task_completion.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Keyboard Emulation mode is enabled in bcWebCam
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam interface or settings menu shows that 'Keyboard Emulation' (or keyboard wedge output mode) is selected and active.

task_completion.description: Microsoft Edge is open to the POS URL
task_completion.visual_evidence: Microsoft Edge is open and the address bar displays 'localhost:8080' or 'http://localhost:8080'.

task_completion.description: The Product Barcode input field is focused
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Product Barcode' text input field on the POS web page is visible and has an active text cursor, indicating it is focused.

task_completion.description: The decoded barcode is entered into the input field
task_completion.visual_evidence: The expected barcode number '5449000000996' is populated inside the 'Product Barcode' input field.
A success message or confirmation screen is displayed in the browser, indicating the item was successfully added after submitting the form.

integrity.description: bcWebCam was actually used for the task
integrity.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam application is visible in the screenshots during the process, confirming the agent did not just manually type the barcode without opening the software.

integrity.description: Interaction occurred on the correct local server
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows the correct local server URL (localhost:8080) and not a local file path (e.g., file://).

integrity.description: Developer tools were not used to fake the success screen
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open in any of the screenshots, ensuring the success screen was not manually manipulated.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected barcode to be scanned and entered is 5449000000996. The expected POS URL is http://localhost:8080.
